key_highlights: U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%, fueling rate-hike fears and a tech sell-off (Nasdaq down 2%).
AI chip stocks sold off sharply; AMD and Marvell fell, while DoubleLine and Oaktree brace for potential AI pain.
Container rates from Asia to US spiked 109% since Iran war started, signaling severe supply chain disruption.
SpaceX IPO draws oversubscribed orders at a $1.77 trillion valuation, highlighting investor appetite for high-growth tech.
Fed officials (Daly, Barr) warned investors about AI stock risks and looser bank rules, adding to market uncertainty.
UK high street chain closures and charity shop struggles reflect consumer strain in high-tax Britain.
Goldman Sachs raised PT on HPE, Wedbush on PANW, Piper Sandler on GOOGL, Wells Fargo on INTC and MSFT, indicating selective bullishness on tech.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off in AI chip stocks (AMD, Marvell, Broadcom) amid rate-hike fears and profit-taking after strong May gains. Fed's Daly bluntly warned AI stock investors. DoubleLine and Oaktree brace for potential AI pain. However, analysts raised PTs on HPE, PANW, GOOGL, INTC, MSFT, suggesting selective opportunities.
sectors.implications: Short-term volatility likely; investors should differentiate between overhyped AI names and those with solid fundamentals. AI infrastructure spending remains robust (data centers, satellites), but sentiment is fragile.

sectors.trend: UK high street chain closures and charity shop struggles indicate consumer spending pressure in high-tax environment. Americans face mortgage rate decisions. Black Rock Coffee Bar targets 1,000 stores, showing selective expansion. Broadway eyes London with tax credits.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary stocks may face headwinds in regions with high inflation/taxes. Defensive plays (Coca-Cola, Costco) remain favored. Mortgage-sensitive sectors (housing, home improvement) need monitoring.

sectors.trend: Oil markets stop believing Trump's peace narrative; Iran war approaches 100 days. Asia-to-US container rates up 109%. Jet fuel costs rising (IATA). Gasoline prices down 17 cents nationally. Dangote boosts local petrol supplies.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium in oil remains elevated. Shipping and logistics costs will pressure margins. Energy stocks may benefit from higher oil prices, but volatility is high.

sectors.trend: Frasers weighs £500M bid for shopping center. Mortgage rate news affects American homeowners. Rayonier's wood products segment, Weyerhaeuser Q1 outlook, and Mohawk adapting to market pressures.
sectors.implications: Commercial real estate selective recovery; residential housing sensitive to rates. Timber/wood products face demand uncertainty. REITs with strong balance sheets may offer value.

sectors.trend: Eli Lilly's $3.8B vaccine bets. Alvotech receives FDA Form 483. Senseonics shareholder call. Flesh-eating screwworm returns to US threatening cattle herd.
sectors.implications: Pharma/biotech M&A and vaccine development remain active. Regulatory risks persist (Alvotech). Agricultural biosecurity becomes a new theme.

sectors.trend: Container rates spike 109% since Iran war. IATA warns of air transport stagflation. Dycom builds fake town to train data center workers. SpaceX IPO oversubscribed.
sectors.implications: Supply chain disruption is acute; logistics companies may see margin pressure but also pricing power. SpaceX IPO highlights space/logistics innovation.

sectors.trend: Fed's Barr warns of risks tied to looser Wall Street bank rules. Susquehanna builds prediction markets business. Family offices bet on sports. Raizen $13B out-of-court deal.
sectors.implications: Banking sector faces regulatory uncertainty; prediction markets and alternative investments gain traction. Family office interest in sports assets is a growing trend.

key_themes: Rate-hike fears dominate after strong jobs report, pressuring growth stocks.
AI stock rotation: profit-taking in high-flyers, selective upgrades by analysts.
Geopolitical risk: Iran war, US sanctions on Cuba, China regulatory overhaul, Bolivia unrest.
Supply chain disruption: container rates spike, jet fuel costs rise, data center buildout accelerates.
Consumer strain: UK high street closures, mortgage rate decisions, charity shop struggles.
Space race: SpaceX IPO oversubscribed, satellite focus in AI era.
Regulatory shifts: Fed warns on bank rules, China fund overhaul, US tariffs on forced labor.
Sports & entertainment: World Cup 2026 preparations, family office bets, Broadway expansion.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor value/defensive stocks; hold cash for dip-buying opportunities.
Take partial profits on high-flying AI names; diversify into non-AI tech and other sectors; focus on companies with strong earnings and cash flows.
Increase exposure to energy and defense stocks; hedge with commodities; avoid shipping/logistics stocks with high Iran exposure.
Invest in companies with nearshoring/reshoring strategies; favor logistics firms with pricing power; monitor container rate trends.
Reduce exposure to discretionary retail; favor staples (KO, COST) and discount retailers; monitor mortgage rate impact on housing.
Diversify across sectors; avoid overconcentration in heavily regulated industries; stay informed on Fed, SEC, and trade policy changes.

opportunities.recommendation: Selectively buy AI infrastructure plays on pullback (e.g., HPE, PANW, GOOGL, MSFT) given analyst upgrades and long-term demand.
opportunities.rationale: Goldman, Wedbush, Piper Sandler, and Wells Fargo raised PTs on these names, indicating strong fundamentals despite sector sell-off. AI data center buildout is secular.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy and defense stocks to hedge geopolitical risk from Iran war.
opportunities.rationale: Oil markets skeptical of peace narrative; defense spending likely to increase. Caterpillar (CAT) raised by UBS benefits from infrastructure and energy demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider SpaceX IPO allocation if available, given oversubscribed demand and $1.77T valuation.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX is a leader in space logistics and satellite internet; IPO frenzy suggests strong aftermarket performance. Use VTI/VOO for indirect exposure.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to defensive consumer staples (KO, COST) amid consumer spending uncertainty.
opportunities.rationale: Coca-Cola exploring India listing, Costco reiterated buy by Morgan Stanley. Staples provide stability in a rate-hike environment.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore prediction markets and alternative assets (sports, private equity) as family offices do.
opportunities.rationale: Susquehanna building prediction markets; family offices betting on sports. Diversification into non-correlated assets can reduce portfolio volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta AI stocks and take profits on names like AMD, Marvell, and Broadcom.
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed's Daly blunt warning, DoubleLine/Oaktree bracing for AI pain, and broad tech sell-off suggest further downside risk. Rotate to quality.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation or short-term Treasuries to prepare for potential rate hike.
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong jobs report reduces likelihood of Fed cuts; Chair Warsh faces policy tests. Cash provides optionality to buy dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against supply chain disruption by shorting shipping stocks or buying commodity futures.
defensive_moves.rationale: Container rates up 109% since Iran war; IATA warns of stagflation. Supply chain bottlenecks will persist.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid UK high street retail and commercial real estate exposed to consumer weakness.
defensive_moves.rationale: Charity shops can't survive in high-tax Britain; major chain closures. Frasers bid may be opportunistic but sector headwinds remain.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor mortgage rate news and reduce exposure to homebuilders and housing-related stocks.
defensive_moves.rationale: Americans face decision after mortgage rate news; higher rates will pressure housing demand. Mohawk, Weyerhaeuser, LPX all facing headwinds.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets are likely to remain volatile over the next 1-3 months as the Fed signals a potential rate hike. Tech stocks, especially AI names, may face further profit-taking. Geopolitical risks from the Iran war and supply chain disruptions will keep energy and shipping costs elevated. Investors should favor defensive sectors (staples, healthcare) and quality tech with strong analyst support.
market_outlook.long_term: Over 6-12 months, the secular growth themes of AI, space, and data center infrastructure remain intact. The SpaceX IPO and continued AI capex (Dycom's training facility, Convalt's $6.2B project) underscore long-term demand. However, higher-for-longer interest rates and geopolitical fragmentation could cap upside. Diversification across regions (Asia ex-Japan, US value) and asset classes (commodities, private equity) is prudent.
key_catalysts: Fed meeting and rate decision in June/July 2026.
Iran war developments and potential ceasefire or escalation.
SpaceX IPO pricing and first-day trading performance.
Q2 earnings season, especially for AI-related companies (NVDA, AMD, MSFT).
US presidential election campaign and trade policy announcements.
China regulatory overhaul and stimulus measures.
World Cup 2026 preparations and related economic impact.
Inflation data (CPI, PPI) for May and June.
watch_list: Fed funds futures and rate hike probability.
Container rate index (FBX, WCI) for supply chain stress.
Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and geopolitical risk premium.
AI stock volatility (NVDA, AMD, MRVL, AVGO).
US dollar index (DXY) and emerging market currency stability.
UK consumer confidence and retail sales data.
SpaceX IPO subscription updates and lock-up periods.
Eli Lilly vaccine trial results and FDA decisions.
CrowdStrike (CRWD) earnings and cybersecurity spending trends.
